content.problemStatement:
You are given a **doubly linked list** of integers. Your task is to **reverse the list in-place** and print the elements of the reversed list.

Here is the **same style of function-declaration explanation**, rewritten for your **reverse doubly linked list** problem:

Function Declaration**Function Name**reverseDLLreverseDLLreverseDLL — This function reverses the given doubly linked list by swapping the `next` and `prev` pointers of each node.

Parametersheadheadhead: A pointer to the **first node** of the original doubly linked list.


If `head` is `NULL`, the list is empty.
Return ValueReturns a pointer to the **new head** of the doubly linked list after reversal.


If the list is empty, returns `NULL`.
`The input and output formats provided below are only for testing with custom inputs.`

Constraints:
1≤T≤1001 \le T \le 1001≤T≤100
1≤N≤1061 \le N \le 10^{6}1≤N≤106
0≤node.val≤1040 \le \text{node.val} \le 10^{4}0≤node.val≤104
Note: The sum of N over all test cases will not exceed 106.\textbf{Note: } \text{The sum of } N \text{ over all test cases will not exceed } 10^{6}.Note: The sum of N over all test cases will not exceed 106.

Input Format
The first line contains an integer **T**, the number of test cases.


For each test case:



The first line contains an integer **N**, the number of nodes in the list.
The second line contains **N** space-separated integers representing the values of the nodes from head to tail.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing **N** space-separated integers representing the reversed list.
constraints: 1 <= N <= 10^6 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^6)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (the number of nodes). The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the node values from head to tail.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line with N space‑separated integers representing the list after it has been reversed.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Three test cases are processed. Each list is reversed, producing the shown sequences.
A single list of five increasing numbers is reversed to decreasing order.
First list becomes 30 20 10. Second list contains identical values, so reversal leaves it unchanged.

solution.approach: For each test case we build the doubly linked list using the given values. To reverse the list we traverse it once, swapping the next and prev pointers of every node. While swapping we keep a reference to the current node; after the loop finishes this reference points to the new head of the list. The algorithm works in O(N) time and O(1) extra space because we only modify existing pointers.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of nodes in that test case. Overall O(total N) ≤ O(10^6).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (the list nodes themselves occupy O(N) memory).

code.Python3:
import sys

def reverseDLL(head):
    cur = head
    new_head = None
    while cur:
        nxt = cur.next
        cur.next, cur.prev = cur.prev, nxt
        new_head = cur
        cur = nxt
    return new_head

class Node:
    __slots__ = ('val','next','prev')
    def __init__(self, v):
        self.val = v
        self.next = None
        self.prev = None

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        head = tail = None
        for _ in range(N):
            v = int(next(it))
            nd = Node(v)
            if head is None:
                head = tail = nd
            else:
                tail.next = nd
                nd.prev = tail
                tail = nd
        head = reverseDLL(head)
        vals = []
        cur = head
        while cur:
            vals.append(str(cur.val))
            cur = cur.next
        out_lines.append(' '.join(vals))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
